content:
Most Angular developers write code that works. Senior Angular developers write code that lasts.

3 min read
1 hour ago

--

That is the difference. That is the line between mid-level and senior. And most developers never cross it.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you are serious about becoming the kind of Angular engineer who gets trusted with architecture decisions, who gets called in when things break, and who earns respect in code reviews, then you need to stop ignoring these habits.
1. Thinking in Architecture
Junior developers think in components. Seniors think in systems.
A senior Angular developer does not just ask, “Does this component work?” They ask, “How will this component interact with the rest of the application six months from now?”
Code
App
├── CoreModule
├── SharedModule
├── FeatureModule
├── components
├── services
└── modelsThis separation is not decoration. It is survival.
2. Writing Predictable State Management
Most developers sprinkle BehaviorSubject and EventEmitter everywhere. Seniors know that chaos begins when state is…
